raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The legal defense fund allocates $25 million for the California Department of Justice to protect California taxpayers and the state’s economy.",
    "Immigrants in California contribute over $1 trillion yearly to the California economy.",
    "The recent memo from the Office of Management and Budget raised serious questions about the potential impacts of abrupt cuts in federal funding.",
    "California immigrants pay nearly $130 billion annually in state and federal taxes.",
    "88% of farmworkers in California are immigrants, approximately half of whom are undocumented.",
    "Immigrants hold one-third of all tourism and hospitality jobs in California.",
    "Immigrants make up 41% of the construction workforce in California.",
    "50% of certified nursing assistants in California nursing homes are immigrants.",
    "The Department of Justice spent approximately $40 million on legal defense to protect billions of dollars for California between 2016 and 2021.",
    "Successful defenses included ensuring the Department of Energy could finalize efficiency rules, generating more than $8 billion in savings for ratepayers.",
    "The number of positions dedicated to fire protection at CalFire increased from 5,756 to 10,275, an approximately 80% increase between 2014 and 2024.",
    "CalFire's total base wildfire protection budget has nearly tripled over the past 10 years, from $1.1 billion in 2014-15 to $3 billion in 2023-24.",
    "The 2021 and 2022 climate packages included more than $2.8 billion in investments for wildfire and forest resilience.",
    "The 24-25 budget maintains $200 million through 2028 for prevention programs focused on prescribed burns and fuels reductions."
  ]
}
```
